text: Sent, after Nabuchodonosor had returned from Egypt. (The year of the world 3415.) This consultation should be placed after Jeremias 37. (Calmet)<hr>Phassur, specified above, and Jeremias 38:1., (Calmet) or a different person. (St. Jerome) (Piscator)<hr>Sophonias, the second priest, (chap. 37:3., and 52:24.) of the 24th class, (1 Paralipomenon 24:18.) who was slain at Reblatha. (Calmet)<hr>Jeremias threatens the great ones to Jeremias 29. and is persecuted. He spoke this when Nabuchodonosor invaded the country. (Worthington)
City. The people shall turn their arms against each other; or rather the Chaldeans shall use their weapons to destroy them. (Calmet)
Pestilence. Septuagint, "death;" the sword, and various diseases. (Haydock)
To them. This was verified, Jeremias 52., and 4 Kings xxiv.
Death; the former if they submit, the latter if they fight, ver. 9. (Calmet)<hr>God's grace is ever ready, that sinners may be converted. (Worthington)
Spoil. He shall be happy to escape naked, Jeremias 38:3. (Calmet)

Valley. He speaks to Jerusalem, confiding in the strength of her situation, upon rocks, surrounded with a deep valley. (Challoner)<hr>Literally, "valley, solid and in a plain." (Haydock)<hr>Isaias (xxii. 1.) styles it ironically, "the valley of vision." (Calmet)<hr>Jerusalem was situated on many rocks. (Josephus, Jewish Wars 6:6.)

Doings. Hebrew, "studies." So "Your studious pursuits" occurs, ver. 12, in many Hebrew manuscripts, though the printed copy have, "their;" and "spoiled" for apprised. (Kennicott)<hr>Forest, the temple, which is called Libanus, (Zacharias 11:21.) and the houses built of cedar-wood, (4 Kings 25:9.; Calmet) particularly the royal palace of the forest, 3 Kings vii. (Haydock)
